title: Project Report: User Management REST API — Java Backend Developer
author: Gagan Pasupuleti
summary: Spring Boot 3 CRUD API with JPA, validation, OpenAPI, and Docker Compose + PostgreSQL.
objectives: Design REST endpoints with proper HTTP status codes
Persist User entity with unique email
source_file: CodeQuest project specification (official docs + industry patterns)

chapters.title: Project Overview
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Project Overview** *(Level: Beginner)*

**User Management REST API** is a beginner-level end-to-end project for Java Backend Developer. Duration: 2–3 weeks. Spring Boot 3 CRUD API with JPA, validation, OpenAPI, and Docker Compose + PostgreSQL. Tools: Java 21, Spring Boot 3, PostgreSQL, Docker.

Code Reference:
```text
Objective: Design REST endpoints with proper HTTP status codes
Objective: Persist User entity with unique email
Objective: Write JUnit + MockMvc tests
Objective: Containerize with Flyway migrations
```
What it shows: Lists measurable outcomes before you start building.

### What it actually is
A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.

### When to use it
At project kickoff.

### Where to use it
Java Backend Developer portfolios and CodeQuest project submissions.

### Real use example
A candidate lists this project on a Java Backend Developer resume with a demo link.
key_takeaways: A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.
A candidate lists this project on a Java Backend Developer resume with a demo link.

chapters.title: Phase: Scaffold
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: Scaffold** *(Level: Beginner)*

Initialize Spring Boot with web, JPA, validation. Complete these tasks: Create User entity; Configure PostgreSQL. Deliverable for this phase: Health endpoint running.

Code Reference:
```text
- [ ] Create User entity
- [ ] Configure PostgreSQL
```
What it shows: Checklist format helps track phase completion.

### What it actually is
Phase Scaffold is one step in the full project lifecycle.

### When to use it
During week 1 of the project timeline.

### Where to use it
Java Backend Developer agile sprints and coursework milestones.

### Real use example
Team demo shows the Health endpoint running to the teacher.

chapters.title: Phase: REST Layer
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: REST Layer** *(Level: Beginner)*

Build controller, service, DTO layers. Complete these tasks: CRUD endpoints; Global exception handler. Deliverable for this phase: Postman collection all green.

Code Reference:
```text
- [ ] CRUD endpoints
- [ ] Global exception handler
```

chapters.title: Phase: Testing
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: Testing** *(Level: Beginner)*

Unit and integration tests. Complete these tasks: @WebMvcTest; @DataJpaTest. Deliverable for this phase: 80%+ coverage report.

Code Reference:
```text
- [ ] @WebMvcTest
- [ ] @DataJpaTest
```
What it shows: Checklist format helps track phase completion.

Phase Testing is one step in the full project lifecycle.

During week 3 of the project timeline.

chapters.title: Final Deliverables and Review
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Final Deliverables and Review** *(Level: Beginner)*

Submit all final deliverables: GitHub repo; OpenAPI spec; Test report; Demo video. Skills demonstrated: Spring Boot, JPA, REST, Docker. Prepare a 5-minute walkthrough video and README for CodeQuest teacher marking.

Code Reference:
```text
Deliverable: GitHub repo
Deliverable: OpenAPI spec
Deliverable: Test report
Deliverable: Demo video
```
What it shows: Final checklist ensures nothing is missing at submission.

### What it actually is
Deliverable review confirms end-to-end project completion.

### When to use it
Before deadline and teacher review.

### Where to use it
CodeQuest project gallery and interviews.

### Real use example
A student submits GitHub repo and passes the rubric.
